subject: app-owned-lan-developer-coordinator-lifecycle
implementationClaims: The primary installed app can persistently opt in to hosting the private LAN developer coordinator without keeping a PowerShell window open.
App startup launches the coordinator before the local outbound agent, and app shutdown stops the agent before the coordinator.
The coordinator exposes only an authenticated loopback MCP attachment in addition to its explicit private agent listener.
Graceful shutdown closes agent and control listeners plus active control sockets; Rust waits for exit and uses a bounded kill-and-reap fallback.
The repository MCP host attaches to an app-owned coordinator when present and retains its previous self-owned fallback for diagnostics.
nonClaims: The new build has not yet been installed and exercised on both physical computers.
The LAN control plane does not itself synchronize research data.
Node.js remains a prerequisite on the computer hosting this development-mode coordinator.
The pairing key authenticates an installation connection; it is not human identity, roles, or per-tool authorization.
No public-network exposure, automatic firewall mutation, discovery broadcast, or key rotation UI is claimed.
